insight: When a plan reduces scope by replacing a broken path with a new one, the old path stays in the code unless explicitly deleted. Always search for call sites of the deprecated path (grep --backend OpenClaw) and decide: delete, fix, or explicitly accept the landmine. The "three orphaned call sites" pattern in Phase 2 (territory_game.py, coverage_game.py, openclaw-railway-smoke CI job) is a canonical example.

Gateway-style skills that use a fixed session_prefix (roboclaws-agent) leak MEMORY between runs if the Gateway container is long-running. CI is safe (ephemeral container) but local dev isn't. Fix: derive prefix from int(time.time()) or uuid4().hex[:8] per invocation. Reviewers should flag any session_key that comes from a constant.

roboclaws pins OpenClaw Gateway image to ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:2026.4.14 (user-provided tag as of 2026-04-16). Use this tag in any local Docker run examples for this project. Digest pinning is captured as a follow-up TODO.

Codex CLI hits daily/usage cap with cryptic chatgpt.com/explore/plus message; autoplan degrades cleanly to subagent-only when this happens. Worth checking quota proactively for multi-phase reviews. Rate-limit reset is shown in error: e.g. "try again at Apr 22nd, 2026".

When a plan has 2+ high-severity unknowns (e.g. 'does vendor long-poll?' + 'what's the tool format?'), insert a 2-hour pre-build spike task that answers both with throwaway code. Saves rewriting T50-T54 after the shape is wrong. Gate: no spec-defined code until spike answers land.

For agent-driven simulation replays, distinguish 'frames the agent saw' (via observe tool) from 'frames captured server-side during moves'. Store both in the trace with a seen_by_agent bool and visualize them differently in the HTML report (green+eye vs grey+walker). Makes the agent's observation cadence legible at a glance and surfaces over/under-observing as a visible metric.

When adding a long-running method (e.g. start_run with 10-min wall-budget) alongside an existing short-timeout method (step with 180s default) on the same class, DO NOT mutate self._client.timeout. Use per-call timeout= override. Otherwise every existing caller silently inherits the long timeout. This is a regression waiting to happen — add a test that calls long-method then short-method and asserts the short one still honors its default.

skills/ai2thor-navigator/SKILL.md hardcodes 10+ MCP response field names that are the de-facto ABI for the OpenClaw agent. Any simplification of mcp_server.py must grep-check these field names still exist. Mock tests cannot catch field renames. Add SKILL.md sync step to any mcp_server.py refactor plan.

In OpenClaw interactive chat, a chat-tail ending with an openclaw prompt-error followed by an empty assistant message with stopReason aborted means the Gateway embedded run hit agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds. Check the container session JSONL plus tmp openclaw log first, then compare the live make and python process against the current Makefile because local transient chat targets can leave a running Gateway seeded with a different provider or model than HEAD.

OpenClaw Gateway image 2026.4.24 can take just over 60s to become ready while installing bundled runtime deps. If make chat exits at READY_TIMEOUT before ready, the host-side Roboclaws MCP server closes but the Gateway container can keep serving the Control UI, leaving the agent with only session_status and no roboclaws navigation tools. Use READY_TIMEOUT=180+ and clean up the container on bootstrap failure.
